tasks.name: Wikipedia: Python release year
tasks.prompt: Load the agent-browser skill and connect to port 9222. Then immediately navigate to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language). Read the infobox and report: (1) the year Python was first released, (2) the name of its creator.

tasks.name: GitHub: facebook/react star count
tasks.prompt: Load the agent-browser skill and connect to port 9222. Navigate to https://github.com/facebook/react. Find and report the number of GitHub stars this repository has.

tasks.name: Google: current time in Tokyo
tasks.prompt: Load the agent-browser skill and connect to port 9222. Navigate to https://www.google.com and search for "current time in Tokyo". Report the current time shown in the search results.

tasks.name: Form fill: httpbin.org post
tasks.prompt: Load the agent-browser skill and connect to port 9222. Navigate to https://httpbin.org/forms/post. Fill in the form with: Customer name = "Test User", Telephone = "1234567890", Pizza size = Large. Submit the form and report what appears on the result page. Save a screenshot to eval-form-result.png before finishing.

tasks.name: Screenshot: example.com
tasks.prompt: Load the agent-browser skill and connect to port 9222. Navigate to https://example.com. Take a screenshot and save it to eval-screenshot.png. Report the page title.

tasks.name: YouTube: open a Minecraft video
tasks.prompt: Load the agent-browser skill and connect to port 9222. Navigate to https://www.youtube.com and search for "Minecraft". Click on the first video in the search results to open it. Save a screenshot to eval-minecraft.png once the video is open. Report the title of the video you opened and confirm the video is playing or loaded.

tasks.name: Memory: test fs-memory skill
tasks.prompt: You are testing the fs-memory system. Use the 'mem' CLI tool to do exactly this: 1. Write the string 'EVAL_TEST_SUCCESS_2026' to a new file 'memory/eval-test.md' using 'mem write'. 2. Read it back using 'mem read' to verify. 3. Delete the file using standard bash rm. 4. Append a journal entry using 'mem journal' saying that the memory test completed successfully. Do everything you were just asked and report the exact string you read back.
